## Coverage notes

### 2026-05-27 — PR Newswire

**Kafene and Harry Ritchie's Jewelers Expand Access to Flexible Purchase Options for Jewelry Shoppers**

Kafene has partnered with Harry Ritchie's Jewelers to offer flexible/lease-to-own purchase options in the jewelry vertical — a sector where Snap Finance also competes. This signals Kafene is actively expanding its retail merchant network into specialty jewelry, representing incremental competitive pressure in that segment.

Significance: medium · Category: partnership

### 2026-05-10 — Traction News

**Dan the Tire Man Launches No Credit Needed Program for Tire Industry, Driving Pre-Approved Financing Customers to Local Shops for Same-Day Sales and Installation**

The headline references "Dan the Tire Man" launching a no-credit-needed tire financing program, but the snippet doesn't mention Kafene at all — it's unclear whether Kafene is the underlying financing partner or if this is a separate program entirely. The read-through for Snap is thin without confirmation of Kafene's involvement, though tires/auto is a vertical Snap competes in and same-day pre-approval programs are a direct competitive mechanic.

Significance: low · Category: partnership

### 2026-05-05 — Tire Review Magazine

**Dan the Tire Man Launches No-Credit-Needed Lease-to-Own Program with Kafene**

Dan the Tire Man, a tire retailer, has partnered with Kafene to offer a no-credit-needed lease-to-own program — a direct move into the automotive/tire vertical that overlaps with Snap Finance's addressable merchant base. This signals Kafene is actively expanding its brick-and-mortar retail partnerships in a segment Snap also targets.

Significance: medium · Category: partnership

### 2026-05-05 — Osservatorio Balcani Caucaso Transeuropa

**Interreg-Ristor: revitalise abandoned mountain villages in Greece and Albania**

The headline and snippet describe a European regional development initiative focused on revitalizing abandoned mountain villages in Greece and Albania — this has no discernible connection to Kafene or the point-of-sale/lease-to-own financing industry. There is no actionable competitive intelligence here for Snap Finance.

Significance: low · Category: other
